spec_match.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified exact Container Timing support detection through PerformanceContainerTiming, native observer activation, simulated PerformanceContainerTiming-shaped entries, containertiming attributes on sections, and containertiming-ignore on the lazy ad subtree.

interactive.rationale: The page now exposes simulate, content-visibility toggle, content profile select, delay scale range, ignored-subtree checkbox, reset, lazy injection, inject-all, and existing-content update controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still demonstrates content-visibility integration while explicitly separating native Container Timing support from replayable simulation.

edge_cases.rationale: The ignored lazy ad subtree and existing-content update paths cover two spec edge cases: ignored descendants and updates that should not produce a new container entry.

references.rationale: The labelled references section links to ChromeStatus feature 5110962817073152, the Chrome origin-trial explainer, the Container Timing draft, and related Element Timing material.

design_system.rationale: Button hover background overrides now set colour, controls use CSS variables, and DevTools reported no form-label or contrast issues during the exercised path.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome/151 verified native PerformanceContainerTiming detection, simulation replay, content-visibility toggle, profile/delay controls, lazy injection, containertiming-ignore for an ad subtree, existing-content no-entry messaging, reference links, no console errors, and the feature conformance route at 6 PASS / 0 FAIL.
